page: Amazon Web Services i7i.48xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8559C processors built on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 192 vCPUs, corresponding to 96 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a base frequency of 2.4 GHz and backed by a 320 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing a balanced 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is a primary feature of this configuration, consisting of 45,000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. The server operates on the Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores all placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The DDR5 memory subsystem achieves top-tier read and write bandwidth, reaching 569.6 GB/sec for large-block uncached reads. The CPU architecture includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which contribute to strong performance in specialized workloads, including top-tier results in Passmark encryption and LLM inference text generation for medium and large models. Single-core performance is more moderate, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng single-core tests.

From a resource density perspective, the i7i.48xlarge offers a highly consolidated hardware profile, combining massive local NVMe storage with a high-capacity memory footprint and high-count dedicated vCPUs. This density makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require high-throughput local storage and substantial memory without the overhead of managing multiple smaller instances. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, high-performance Redis deployments, software compilation, and data-intensive applications requiring high-speed local storage and 100 Gbps networking.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.48xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8559C processors on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz and a massive 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the server delivers 100 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place this instance in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. While single-core performance is average, its massive parallel processing capabilities and high-speed local storage make it highly efficient for large-scale databases, Redis deployments, and data-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.48xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 192 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1536 GB of DDR5 RAM, 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.48xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 192 Intel vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 45 TB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density, storage-optimized platform combining massive local NVMe capacity with top-tier multi-core processing and high-speed DDR5 memory.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with AVX-512 and AMX support
1536 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
45,000 GB of high-throughput local NVMe SSD storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Best suited for large-scale databases, Redis, and data-intensive workloads
